 {"id":"bd-3mtq","title":"Unit Tests: extract_buffer_row Overflow Prevention","description":"Test the saturating_add overflow prevention in extract_buffer_row.

## Test Cases
1. `test_extract_normal_range` - typical start_x/max_len within bounds
2. `test_extract_saturating_at_max` - start_x=u32::MAX-10, max_len=20 saturates
3. `test_extract_empty_on_overflow` - returns empty when range saturates
4. `test_extract_at_buffer_edge` - start_x at buffer width boundary

## Files
- src/bin/demo_showcase.rs: extract_buffer_row()

 {"id":"bd-3nbd","title":"EPIC: Text/Editor System Fixes","description":"## Overview

This epic addresses **TEXT EDITING AND SYNTAX HIGHLIGHTING BUGS** discovered during the deep codebase audit.

## Critical Issues

### CRLF Handling (HIGH)
The syntax highlighter tokenizer assumes LF-only line endings. CRLF files have completely misaligned highlighting because offset calculation adds +1 per line instead of +2.

### Tab Selection (HIGH)
When a tab character expands to multiple columns (e.g., 4 spaces), selection highlighting only applies to the first column. The other columns don't show the selection style.

### Visual Column Calculation (MEDIUM)
Vertical navigation (up/down) in wrapped text calculates the wrong target column, causing cursor jumps.

## Other Issues

- Cursor positioning with multi-byte characters
- Undo/redo memory efficiency (string cloning)
- Line ending style not tracked (CRLF vs LF)
- EOL detection fragile for CR-only files

## Impact

- CRLF files look broken (most common issue)
- Selection is confusing with tabs
- Wrapped text is hard to navigate

## Background

The text system uses:
- Ropey crate for efficient text storage
- Custom tokenizer for syntax highlighting
- Virtual line system for text wrapping
- EditBuffer for undo/redo

Most issues are in the integration between these components.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:06:51.117889003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:29:28.013249910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:29:28.013185470Z","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: CRLF handling, visual column calculation, and tab selection highlighting.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3nbv","title":"E2E tests: Input handling and event flow","description":"# E2E Test: Input Handling & Event Flow

## Scope
Test complete input handling from raw bytes to parsed events to application response.

## Test Scenarios

### Keyboard Input Flow
- [ ] Single keypress → KeyEvent
- [ ] Modifier combinations (Ctrl+X, Alt+Y, etc.)
- [ ] Function keys (F1-F12)
- [ ] Special keys (arrows, home, end, etc.)
- [ ] Unicode character input

### Mouse Input Flow
- [ ] Click → MouseEvent with position
- [ ] Drag sequence (press → move → release)
- [ ] Scroll wheel events
- [ ] Mouse position accuracy

### Paste Input
- [ ] Bracketed paste detection
- [ ] Large paste handling
- [ ] Binary data in paste

### Event to Render Response
- [ ] Keypress → buffer change → present
- [ ] Mouse click → hit test → response
- [ ] Resize event → buffer resize → present

### Logging Points
```jsonl
{"step": "input_raw", "bytes": [27, 91, ...]}
{"step": "parse", "event": {"type": "key", "code": "A", "modifiers": []}}
{"step": "handle", "action": "insert_char", "char": "A"}
{"step": "render", "changed_cells": 1}
```

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Partial escape sequences (interrupted input)
- [ ] Rapid input flood
- [ ] Invalid/malformed sequences
- [ ] Timeout handling","notes":"Merged into bd-1nfp (E2E: Input Simulation for Complex Workflows). The input flow testing scenarios and logging points from this bead have been incorporated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:59.535467058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:04.569331009Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:04.569188423Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","input","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nbv","depends_on_id":"bd-2vka","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:59.557573540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nvn","title":"Implement get_visual_line_start() - Visual line start position","description":"# Task: Implement get_visual_line_start()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_visual_line_start(&self) -> usize
```

## Purpose
Returns the byte position at the start of the current visual line. Used for Home key behavior in wrapped text.

## Implementation Notes
- Find where current visual line begins
- Different from logical line start if wrapped
- Requires wrap width context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns correct position
- [ ] Handles wrapped lines
- [ ] Handles unwrapped lines

## Testing
- Unwrapped line (same as logical start)
- Middle of wrapped line","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:24.427576390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:24.440517683Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nvn","depends_on_id":"bd-2dyx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:30:24.440490132Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3o0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: tour mode driver (script executor + deterministic timing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Tour Mode Driver (Script Executor + Deterministic Timing)

## Purpose

Tour mode is how we get:

- unattended demos (screen recordings)
- deterministic behavior for tests/smoke runs
- a guided narrative that proves features in a clear order

This bead implements the runtime that executes the tour script defined in `bd-1gy`.

## Behaviors

- CLI flag --tour starts tour immediately.
- Tour runs a fixed sequence of steps:
  - changes focus/panels
  - triggers overlays (command palette/help)
  - scrolls lists/logs
  - types/pastes into editor
  - triggers preview animations / errors / recovery

Determinism:

- no randomness
- timing driven by a monotonic clock + fixed durations
- any synthetic inputs are generated by the tour driver

Automatic termination (for recordings/E2E):

- If `--exit-after-tour` is enabled (see `bd-3vjm` / `bd-2iv`), the app should exit 0 when the final tour step completes.
- `--max-frames` acts as a hard safety bound; it should never be required for a normal tour, but it prevents hangs.

## Interactive + Headless

The tour driver must work in BOTH:

- interactive mode (real renderer)
- headless mode (for CI regression tests)

Headless mode should be able to execute the tour and emit JSON markers that tests can snapshot (see `bd-bqd1`).

## Implementation Approach

- Introduce a TourRunner in the demo binary:
  - state: TourState { step_idx, step_started_at, paused, ... }
  - fn tick(now, app, out_actions)

- The tour runner should not directly draw; it only manipulates app state and injects actions.

- When the tour wants to show something, it should set a TourHighlight { rect, message } that the tour overlay bead renders.

## Acceptance Notes

- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour produces a stable “demo story” without user input.
- Tour can be paused/resumed and cleanly exited.
- Tour never leaves the app in a broken focus/input state when it ends.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process terminates cleanly at completion.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour` produces a complete, deterministic feature walkthrough without user input.
- Tour driving works in both interactive mode and headless mode (headless emits stable tour step markers in JSON).
- Tour controls work: next/prev, pause/resume, and exit without leaving focus/input in a broken state.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process exits 0 when the final step completes and the terminal is restored cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.030533207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666987869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666921105Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.254927712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.042919278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.993258401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.262929423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:17.664069555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
